$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:divergence block (Reader-Persona Simulation). One block per beat/finding where the predicted reader experience DIFFERS across personas — divergence is the diagnostic signal, exactly as contract-mismatch is. `anchor` is a finding id (F-...) or a Timeline locus that GROUNDS the prediction — every divergence must cite a manuscript-grounded anchor (validator D2, the signature firewall gate; an ungrounded prediction is a fabricated one). `experiences` is a NESTED map (persona-id -> one of engaged | neutral | friction | disengage); the subset engine cannot descend into nested objects (it types the container as object), so per-element enum membership is checked in persona_divergence.py (the retcon_plan.scores precedent). `magnitude` is the spread across personas. `asserted_severity` is OPTIONAL (Must-Fix | Should-Fix | Could-Fix) and present only if the map restates the anchored finding's severity for an audience — it is the field D3 reads: a divergence may NOT assert a severity LOWER than the anchored finding's locked Ledger severity (segmentation cannot downgrade the target-audience verdict — the anti-persona-shopping guard). Validated by scripts/persona_divergence.py. See docs/reader-persona-simulation.md.
title: APODICTIC persona divergence (apodictic.divergence.v1)

experiences.$comment: Nested persona-id -> experience enum (engaged | neutral | friction | disengage). The subset engine only types the container as object; persona_divergence.py checks each value's enum membership (D1) and that each key resolves to a declared persona.
